Name: Triple S Ranch
BYOV: YES - full flexibility; no vendor fees or surcharges
Kitchen Grade: Commercial - real gas burners; proper ventilation; prep space
On-Site Lodging: YES - up to 60 guests (cabins; treehouses; Victorian farmhouse; bunk rooms)
Outdoor Ceremony: YES - 175-year walnut tree; stone patios; Event Barn; pizza oven; Argentine BBQ
Fire Ceremony: Ask - high fire severity zone (Calistoga). Confirm with Napa County Fire Marshal. Budget $500-$1K for fire safety officer
Noise Curfew: Flexible - 20 acres rural; confirm hard stop time in writing
Baraat Path: Excellent - ranch roads; 20 acres; horse-friendly terrain
Haldi Safe: Caution - ranch lawn manageable with tarps/ground protection. Budget $300-$500 cleanup
Multi-Day: YES - weekend buyout available ($45K full buyout)
Accessibility: Moderate - uneven ranch terrain. Budget $500-$800 for golf cart shuttle for elderly
Indian Wedding Experience: YES - has hosted Indian weddings; commercial kitchen proven for Indian caterers
Notes: Best overall match IF kitchen passes caterer inspection AND fire permits obtainable. #1 differentiator is commercial-grade kitchen.
Action Items: Tour with your caterer to inspect kitchen; confirm fire permits for your date; get noise cutoff in writing; budget for golf cart shuttle

Fire Ceremony: Likely - fire pits on property; rural 40 acres helps. Confirm with Sonoma County

Haldi Safe: Yes - rural ranch setting; easy to protect areas
Accessibility: Good - established venue with maintained paths
Indian Wedding Experience: Not confirmed - flexible venue; 2am music alone makes it worth touring
Notes: PROMOTED from rank 12. The 2am music cutoff alone makes this worth touring. Combined with full BYOV; prep kitchen; and low site fee; most budget-realistic option for a proper Indian wedding.
Action Items: Tour immediately; confirm caterer prep kitchen specs; verify 2am cutoff in writing; check lodging overflow options in Cloverdale/Healdsburg
